response.justification: Sarah Smith is a versatile, experienced polyglot engineer with strong full-stack and distributed systems credentials, but she does not meet the core minimum qualifications for this role. The most critical gap is C++ experience: the role requires 8 years, and the resume shows approximately 6 months (NI LabWindows, Dec 2014–Jun 2015) plus a bachelor thesis project — a disqualifying shortfall. Beyond C++, her career trajectory is oriented toward web application development, TypeScript/Node.js product work, and open-source tooling rather than cloud compute infrastructure, hardware architecture, or platform engineering at the required depth. There is no evidence of 5 years in large-scale infrastructure or distributed systems leadership, nor of a technical lead role specifically mentoring cloud or DevOps engineers.
strengths: Genuine high-throughput distributed systems exposure at Sociomantic, handling millions of HTTP requests per second with low-latency DLang services
Staff-level and founding experience demonstrates initiative and some degree of technical leadership, including workshops and public talks
Broad polyglot background (TypeScript, DLang, C#, Java, Node.js, Kubernetes, AWS CDK) shows adaptability and systems thinking
concerns: C++ experience is approximately 6 months versus the 8-year hard minimum — the single most important technical requirement for this role
No evidence of cloud compute platform ownership, production cloud architecture at scale, or infrastructure leadership in a dedicated platform engineering context
Career history is primarily web and application development; no mentoring of cloud or DevOps engineers and no architectural design at the infrastructure layer
